sentences.zh: 孙子曰：兵者，国之大事，死生之地，存亡之道，不可不察也。
sentences.pinyin: Sūnzǐ yuē: bīng zhě, guó zhī dàshì, sǐ shēng zhī dì, cún wáng zhī dào, bù kě bù chá yě.
sentences.en: Sun Tzu said: War is a great matter of the state — the ground of life and death, the path to survival or ruin. It must be studied with the greatest care.

sentences.zh: 故经之以五事，校之以计，而索其情：一曰道，二曰天，三曰地，四曰将，五曰法。
sentences.pinyin: Gù jīng zhī yǐ wǔ shì, jiào zhī yǐ jì, ér suǒ qí qíng: yī yuē dào, èr yuē tiān, sān yuē dì, sì yuē jiàng, wǔ yuē fǎ.
sentences.en: Therefore weigh it by five factors and assess by calculation, to grasp its true condition: first the Way, second Heaven, third Earth, fourth the General, fifth Method.
War is the way of deception.

sentences.pinyin: Gōng qí wú bèi, chū qí bù yì.
sentences.en: Attack where they are unprepared; appear where you are not expected.

sentences.zh: 夫未战而庙算胜者，得算多也。
sentences.pinyin: Fú wèi zhàn ér miào suàn shèng zhě, dé suàn duō yě.
sentences.en: He who, before the battle is fought, wins through council in the temple, has reckoned the most.

idioms.meaning: Sun Tzu's five fundamental factors of war — a framework Chinese strategists, businesspeople, and military leaders still invoke today when assessing any contest.
idioms.origin: These five categories open The Art of War and became the canonical lens for evaluating strategic situations in Chinese thought.

idioms.meaning: The principle that warfare is fundamentally about misdirection — appearing weak when strong, near when far. This phrase is the source of countless later sayings about strategic deception.
idioms.origin: One of the most-quoted lines in Chinese military philosophy. It marks the shift from rule-bound chivalric warfare to strategic cunning.

idioms.literal: Attack where they have no preparation; emerge where they do not expect
idioms.meaning: A foundational chéngyǔ used to this day in Chinese for any surprise move — in business pitches, sports commentary, even office politics.
idioms.origin: From this very passage of The Art of War. Still cited verbatim 2,500 years later.
